findings.snippet: 4'h0: alu_out = a + b;     // VLG013: blocking in clocked block
findings.suggestion: Change '=' to '<=' for all register assignments in clocked always blocks.

findings.description: 'case' statement missing 'default' in combinational block — latch inferred
findings.suggestion: Add 'default: <output> = <safe_value>;' to every case statement.

findings.description: System task ($display/$monitor/$finish) found in RTL — not synthesizable
findings.snippet: $display("[ALU] Overflow detected! op=%0h a=%0h b=%0h", op, a, b); // VLG011
findings.suggestion: Move system tasks to a testbench file. Guard RTL with `ifdef SIMULATION if needed.

findings.description: 'initial' block found — not synthesizable (simulation-only construct)
findings.suggestion: Remove 'initial' block from RTL. Use reset logic in a clocked always block instead.

findings.description: Combinational feedback loop detected — signal appears on both LHS and RHS of assign
findings.suggestion: 'fb_loop' feeds itself — insert a register (flip-flop) to break the loop: use a clocked always block with '<='.

findings.description: Delay (#N) found in RTL — simulation-only, causes sim/synth mismatch
findings.suggestion: Remove all '#delay' constructs from RTL code. Rely on clock edges for timing.

findings.description: `default_nettype none` not set — implicit net declarations risk
findings.suggestion: Add '`default_nettype none' at the top of every RTL file before any module.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'alu' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: High fan-out signal (appears >16 times on RHS) — may need buffer tree in synthesis
findings.suggestion: Consider adding 'synthesis keep' attribute or manually buffering 'a' to help the synthesis tool build an optimal buffer tree.

findings.description: Continuous 'assign' drives a 'reg'-declared signal — use 'wire' instead
findings.suggestion: Change 'reg partial_sum' declaration to 'wire partial_sum' since it's driven by continuous assignment, not a procedural block.

findings.description: Output not assigned on all paths in combinational block — possible latch
findings.snippet: Signal 'ov_flag' may not be assigned on all paths
findings.suggestion: Add default assignment 'ov_flag = <safe_val>;' at the top of the combinational block.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'result' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Clocked always block has no reset condition — FF powers up in unknown state
findings.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin        // VLG026: no reset
findings.suggestion: Add a reset condition: 'if (rst) <output> <= 0; else ...'. For FPGAs you may use 'initial' as alternative, but ASIC requires explicit reset.

findings.description: Same magic constant appears >3 times — extract to parameter / localparam
findings.suggestion: Replace '32'd0' with a named localparam. Repeated literals are error-prone when specifications change.

findings.description: FSM case statement has no 'default' branch — unreachable state lockup risk
findings.suggestion: Add: 'default: state <= IDLE;' (or safe reset state) to handle illegal state transitions gracefully.

findings.description: FSM has no timeout / watchdog — stuck-state lockup risk
findings.snippet: Module 'bad_fifo' has FSM but no timeout/watchdog signal
findings.suggestion: Add a watchdog counter that resets the FSM to IDLE after N cycles of inactivity. This prevents permanent lockup from SEU or firmware bugs.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: 'casex'/'casez' used — X/Z wildcard matching can mask bugs
findings.suggestion: Replace 'casex'/'casez' with 'case' and add explicit 'default' branch.

findings.description: Chained ternary (>2 deep) — replace with case or named wires for clarity
findings.suggestion: Replace chained ternaries with a case statement or break into named intermediate wires for clarity.

findings.description: Blocking assignment '=' used in clocked always block — use '<=' for FFs
findings.suggestion: Change '=' to '<=' for all register assignments in clocked always blocks.

findings.description: Incomplete 'if' without 'else' in combinational block — latch may be inferred
findings.snippet: always @(*) begin                  // VLG009 candidate: explicit sensitivity
findings.suggestion: Add an 'else' clause covering all outputs, or assign defaults at the top of the block before the if statement.

findings.description: 'initial' block found — not synthesizable (simulation-only construct)
findings.suggestion: Remove 'initial' block from RTL. Use reset logic in a clocked always block instead.

findings.description: `default_nettype none` not set — implicit net declarations risk
findings.suggestion: Add '`default_nettype none' at the top of every RTL file before any module.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'pipeline_hazards' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.snippet: output reg [31:0] result, // VLG002: magic number
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: Output not assigned on all paths in combinational block — possible latch
findings.snippet: Signal 'accum' may not be assigned on all paths
findings.suggestion: Add default assignment 'accum = <safe_val>;' at the top of the combinational block.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: 'casex'/'casez' used — X/Z wildcard matching can mask bugs
findings.suggestion: Replace 'casex'/'casez' with 'case' and add explicit 'default' branch.

findings.description: Clock signal used as data (non-clock position) — potential gated clock hazard
findings.suggestion: 'clk_fast' is being gated combinationally. Use a dedicated ICG (Integrated Clock Gating) cell instead.

findings.description: Clock signal used as data (non-clock position) — potential gated clock hazard
findings.suggestion: 'clk_slow' is being gated combinationally. Use a dedicated ICG (Integrated Clock Gating) cell instead.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'stage1_data' to follow convention, e.g. r_stage1_data (reg) or w_stage1_data (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'stage1_valid' to follow convention, e.g. r_stage1_valid (reg) or w_stage1_valid (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'stage2_product' to follow convention, e.g. r_stage2_product (reg) or w_stage2_product (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'stage2_valid' to follow convention, e.g. r_stage2_valid (reg) or w_stage2_valid (wire).

findings.description: Same magic constant appears >3 times — extract to parameter / localparam
findings.suggestion: Replace '32'h0' with a named localparam. Repeated literals are error-prone when specifications change.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'accum' to follow convention, e.g. r_accum (reg) or w_accum (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'accum_valid' to follow convention, e.g. r_accum_valid (reg) or w_accum_valid (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'result_cdc_a' to follow convention, e.g. r_result_cdc_a (reg) or w_result_cdc_a (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'valid_cdc_a' to follow convention, e.g. r_valid_cdc_a (reg) or w_valid_cdc_a (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'debug_clk_xor' to follow convention, e.g. r_debug_clk_xor (reg) or w_debug_clk_xor (wire).

findings.description: Explicit sensitivity list in combinational block — may be incomplete (use always @(*))
findings.snippet: always @(duty_reg) begin               // VLG009: missing counter from sensitivity
findings.suggestion: Replace explicit sensitivity list with 'always @(*)' or SystemVerilog 'always_comb'.

findings.description: Blocking assignment '=' used in clocked always block — use '<=' for FFs
findings.suggestion: Change '=' to '<=' for all register assignments in clocked always blocks.

findings.description: `default_nettype none` not set — implicit net declarations risk
findings.suggestion: Add '`default_nettype none' at the top of every RTL file before any module.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'pwm_controller' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: High-impedance (1'bz) assigned without explicit tri-state enable
findings.snippet: assign dut_io = 1'bz;  // VLG012: always driving Z — missing oe enable pin
findings.suggestion: Use tri-state pattern: assign out = (oe) ? data : 'bz;

findings.description: Clock signal used as data (non-clock position) — potential gated clock hazard
findings.suggestion: 'clk_cfg' is being gated combinationally. Use a dedicated ICG (Integrated Clock Gating) cell instead.

findings.description: Mixed blocking and non-blocking assignments in same always block
findings.suggestion: Use exclusively '<=' in clocked blocks and '=' in combinational blocks. Never mix.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'pwm_out' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'pwm_n' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'dut_io' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.snippet: reg [7:0] duty_reg;       // VLG003: no prefix convention (r_duty_reg)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'duty_reg' to follow convention, e.g. r_duty_reg (reg) or w_duty_reg (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'counter' to follow convention, e.g. r_counter (reg) or w_counter (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'pwm_r' to follow convention, e.g. r_pwm_r (reg) or w_pwm_r (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'duty_sync' to follow convention, e.g. r_duty_sync (reg) or w_duty_sync (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'gated_cfg_clk' to follow convention, e.g. r_gated_cfg_clk (reg) or w_gated_cfg_clk (wire).

findings.description: Clocked always block has no reset condition — FF powers up in unknown state
findings.snippet: always @(posedge gated_cfg_clk) begin     // Using the glitch-prone gated clock
findings.suggestion: Add a reset condition: 'if (rst) <output> <= 0; else ...'. For FPGAs you may use 'initial' as alternative, but ASIC requires explicit reset.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'duty_display' to follow convention, e.g. r_duty_display (reg) or w_duty_display (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'mixed_reg' to follow convention, e.g. r_mixed_reg (reg) or w_mixed_reg (wire).

findings.description: Security signal driven combinationally — glitch-fault bypass risk
findings.snippet: Security signal 'o_grant' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register 'o_grant' with a flip-flop. Combinational security signals are vulnerable to voltage/EM glitch-fault injection.

findings.description: Security signal driven combinationally — glitch-fault bypass risk
findings.snippet: Security signal 'o_secure_ok' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register 'o_secure_ok' with a flip-flop. Combinational security signals are vulnerable to voltage/EM glitch-fault injection.

findings.description: Hardcoded key / credential in RTL — use OTP or eFuse instead
findings.suggestion: Never hardcode keys in RTL. Load them from OTP/eFuse/key-bus at runtime. Hardcoded keys can be extracted from the bitstream by an attacker.

findings.description: Hardcoded key / credential in RTL — use OTP or eFuse instead
findings.suggestion: Never hardcode keys in RTL. Load them from OTP/eFuse/key-bus at runtime. Hardcoded keys can be extracted from the bitstream by an attacker.

findings.description: Sensitive register not zeroed on reset — cryptographic data-remanence risk
findings.snippet: Sensitive register 'cipher_key' is not zeroed on reset
findings.suggestion: Add 'cipher_key <= 0;' in the reset branch to prevent cryptographic data remanence after context switch.

findings.description: Shift amount may exceed signal width — result is always zero or undefined
findings.snippet: 'shift_reg' is 8 bits wide but shifted by 10
findings.suggestion: The shift amount (10) >= signal width (8). This always produces zero. Reduce the shift or widen the signal.

findings.description: 'case' statement missing 'default' in combinational block — latch inferred
findings.suggestion: Add 'default: <output> = <safe_value>;' to every case statement.

findings.description: `default_nettype none` not set — implicit net declarations risk
findings.suggestion: Add '`default_nettype none' at the top of every RTL file before any module.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'security_ctrl' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: Debug / JTAG port in production RTL — potential attack surface
findings.snippet: Port 'i_jtag_tdi' looks like a debug/JTAG interface
findings.suggestion: Gate debug ports behind a hardware fuse or eFuse-controlled mux. In production silicon, debug access should be permanently disabled.

findings.description: Debug / JTAG port in production RTL — potential attack surface
findings.snippet: Port 'i_jtag_tms' looks like a debug/JTAG interface
findings.suggestion: Gate debug ports behind a hardware fuse or eFuse-controlled mux. In production silicon, debug access should be permanently disabled.

findings.description: Debug / JTAG port in production RTL — potential attack surface
findings.snippet: Port 'i_jtag_tck' looks like a debug/JTAG interface
findings.suggestion: Gate debug ports behind a hardware fuse or eFuse-controlled mux. In production silicon, debug access should be permanently disabled.

findings.description: Output port is never driven (assigned) inside the module
findings.suggestion: Add 'assign o_jtag_tdo = <value>;' or drive it from an always block.

findings.description: Debug / JTAG port in production RTL — potential attack surface
findings.snippet: Port 'o_jtag_tdo' looks like a debug/JTAG interface
findings.suggestion: Gate debug ports behind a hardware fuse or eFuse-controlled mux. In production silicon, debug access should be permanently disabled.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: Memory array never cleared on reset — stale-data leak across contexts
findings.snippet: Memory array 'sec_mem' is never cleared on reset or initialization
findings.suggestion: Add a reset loop: 'for (i=0; i<DEPTH; i=i+1) mem[i] <= 0;' to prevent stale data leakage between secure contexts.

findings.description: Magic number used in signal/port width — use a named parameter instead
findings.suggestion: Replace literal width with a named parameter, e.g. `parameter DATA_WIDTH = 32`.

findings.description: FSM case statement has no 'default' branch — unreachable state lockup risk
findings.suggestion: Add: 'default: state <= IDLE;' (or safe reset state) to handle illegal state transitions gracefully.

findings.description: Control flow nested >3 levels deep — hard to review and verify
findings.suggestion: Flatten deep nesting by extracting sub-blocks into separate always blocks, using case statements, or splitting logic into sub-modules.

findings.description: FSM has no timeout / watchdog — stuck-state lockup risk
findings.snippet: Module 'security_ctrl' has FSM but no timeout/watchdog signal
findings.suggestion: Add a watchdog counter that resets the FSM to IDLE after N cycles of inactivity. This prevents permanent lockup from SEU or firmware bugs.

findings.description: Clock-enable feedback — CE depends on its own registered output, livelock risk
findings.snippet: CE signal 'ce_active' is both the if-guard and assigned inside this clocked block
findings.suggestion: Ensure 'ce_active' has an external re-assertion path (e.g. from a different always block or external input) to avoid livelock.

findings.description: Width mismatch in comparison — implicit extension may hide bugs
findings.suggestion: Explicitly extend the narrower signal or use a sized comparison: {{ {abs(lw-rw)}'b0, narrow_cnt }} to avoid implicit extension.

findings.description: Complex boolean expression (>4 operators) — hard to review, split into named wires
findings.suggestion: Break this expression into named intermediate wires with meaningful names. Example: wire addr_hit = (addr >= BASE) & (addr < BASE + SIZE);

findings.description: Chained ternary (>2 deep) — replace with case or named wires for clarity
findings.suggestion: Replace chained ternaries with a case statement or break into named intermediate wires for clarity.

findings.description: Width mismatch in comparison — implicit extension may hide bugs
findings.suggestion: Explicitly extend the narrower signal or use a sized comparison: {{ {abs(lw-rw)}'b0, i_data }} to avoid implicit extension.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'dummy_sub' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: File contains multiple modules — one module per file for traceability
findings.snippet: Second module 'dummy_sub' in same file (first was 'security_ctrl')
findings.suggestion: Move module 'dummy_sub' to its own file 'dummy_sub.v'. One module per file improves discoverability and version-control clarity.

findings.description: Module has >20 ports — god-module, consider splitting functionality
findings.suggestion: Group related signals into sub-modules or use bus interfaces. Large port lists increase integration errors.

findings.description: Module has >10 always blocks — consider decomposing into sub-modules
findings.suggestion: Group related always blocks into sub-modules. Many blocks in one file make signal tracing difficult.

findings.description: Mixed behavioral + structural styles in one module — pick one for clarity
findings.snippet: Module 'security_ctrl' mixes always blocks and module instantiations
findings.suggestion: Separate structural (instantiation) wrappers from behavioral (always) logic. This reduces cognitive load during code review.

findings.snippet: Output port 'o_rdata' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Clocked always block has no reset condition — FF powers up in unknown state
findings.suggestion: Add a reset condition: 'if (rst) <output> <= 0; else ...'. For FPGAs you may use 'initial' as alternative, but ASIC requires explicit reset.

findings.description: FSM case statement has no 'default' branch — unreachable state lockup risk
findings.suggestion: Add: 'default: state <= IDLE;' (or safe reset state) to handle illegal state transitions gracefully.

findings.description: FSM outputs decoded combinationally — consider registering outputs to prevent glitches
findings.suggestion: Move output assignments to a separate registered always block (Moore output model) to prevent glitches.

findings.description: FSM has no timeout / watchdog — stuck-state lockup risk
findings.snippet: Module 'spi_master' has FSM but no timeout/watchdog signal
findings.suggestion: Add a watchdog counter that resets the FSM to IDLE after N cycles of inactivity. This prevents permanent lockup from SEU or firmware bugs.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'spi_clk_div' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: File contains multiple modules — one module per file for traceability
findings.snippet: Second module 'spi_clk_div' in same file (first was 'spi_master')
findings.suggestion: Move module 'spi_clk_div' to its own file 'spi_clk_div.v'. One module per file improves discoverability and version-control clarity.

findings.description: Mixed behavioral + structural styles in one module — pick one for clarity
findings.snippet: Module 'spi_master' mixes always blocks and module instantiations
findings.suggestion: Separate structural (instantiation) wrappers from behavioral (always) logic. This reduces cognitive load during code review.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'o_sclk' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'o_mosi' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'o_cs_n' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'o_done' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'state' to follow convention, e.g. r_state (reg) or w_state (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'shift_reg' to follow convention, e.g. r_shift_reg (reg) or w_shift_reg (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'bit_idx' to follow convention, e.g. r_bit_idx (reg) or w_bit_idx (wire).

findings.description: Clocked always block has no reset condition — FF powers up in unknown state
findings.snippet: always @(posedge i_clk) begin       // VLG026: no reset branch
findings.suggestion: Add a reset condition: 'if (rst) <output> <= 0; else ...'. For FPGAs you may use 'initial' as alternative, but ASIC requires explicit reset.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'o_clk_en' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Reset used as async but not present in always block sensitivity list
findings.suggestion: Other blocks in this module use async reset. Add reset to this block's sensitivity list or convert to sync-reset style consistently.

findings.description: 'case' statement missing 'default' in combinational block — latch inferred
findings.suggestion: Add 'default: <output> = <safe_value>;' to every case statement.

findings.description: Sequential module lacks scan-enable port (DFT gap — ATPG coverage risk)
findings.snippet: Module 'uart_tx' has FFs but no scan_en/test_en port
findings.suggestion: Add 'input scan_en' port and use it to mux FF data input with scan chain data. Consult your DFT methodology guide for scan insertion flow.

findings.description: Mixed synchronous and asynchronous reset strategy detected in module
findings.snippet: Async reset at line 50, sync reset at line 60
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one reset strategy. Recommended: async assert, synchronous deassert using a 2-FF reset synchronizer.

findings.description: FSM case statement has no 'default' branch — unreachable state lockup risk
findings.suggestion: Add: 'default: state <= IDLE;' (or safe reset state) to handle illegal state transitions gracefully.

findings.description: FSM outputs decoded combinationally — consider registering outputs to prevent glitches
findings.suggestion: Move output assignments to a separate registered always block (Moore output model) to prevent glitches.

findings.description: FSM has no timeout / watchdog — stuck-state lockup risk
findings.snippet: Module 'uart_tx' has FSM but no timeout/watchdog signal
findings.suggestion: Add a watchdog counter that resets the FSM to IDLE after N cycles of inactivity. This prevents permanent lockup from SEU or firmware bugs.

findings.description: Clock signal used as data (non-clock position) — potential gated clock hazard
findings.suggestion: 'clk_uart' is being gated combinationally. Use a dedicated ICG (Integrated Clock Gating) cell instead.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'tx_out' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Unregistered output — combinational path to port, glitch & timing risk
findings.snippet: Output port 'tx_busy' is driven combinationally, never registered
findings.suggestion: Register this output with a flip-flop before driving it to the port. This eliminates glitches and eases timing closure.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'state' to follow convention, e.g. r_state (reg) or w_state (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.snippet: reg [2:0] next_state;     // next state computed in combinational block
findings.suggestion: Rename 'next_state' to follow convention, e.g. r_next_state (reg) or w_next_state (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'shift_reg' to follow convention, e.g. r_shift_reg (reg) or w_shift_reg (wire).

findings.suggestion: Rename 'bit_cnt' to follow convention, e.g. r_bit_cnt (reg) or w_bit_cnt (wire).

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'tx_valid_uart' to follow convention, e.g. r_tx_valid_uart (reg) or w_tx_valid_uart (wire).

findings.description: Clocked always block has no reset condition — FF powers up in unknown state
findings.suggestion: Add a reset condition: 'if (rst) <output> <= 0; else ...'. For FPGAs you may use 'initial' as alternative, but ASIC requires explicit reset.

findings.description: Signal name does not follow prefix convention (i_/o_/r_/w_)
findings.suggestion: Rename 'gated_clk' to follow convention, e.g. r_gated_clk (reg) or w_gated_clk (wire).
